data.body:
PASS 1 review — [T3] Integration feasibility (overlay, applies: plan exercises the real `AcliClient` against live Jira + references Jira Cloud OpenAPI).

VERDICT: PASS with one minor coaching item.

Affirmations (criteria satisfied):
- technical_feasibility: The dual-run shape-contract harness is achievable as described. The live-gating mechanism it depends on already exists in-repo (`tests/external/test_link_sync_live.py`, `tests/external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py`) using the `pytest.mark.external` marker + a `_live_jira_ready()` creds/binary gate — the exact "opt-in, does not flake/slow the default suite" pattern AC4 calls for.
- Named-method existence — MATCH: all four enrichment methods exist on the real client. `get_comment_map`/`get_issuelinks_map` at src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli_graph.py:289 and :391, with documented return shapes (`{jira_key: <comment field dict>}`, `{jira_key: [<issuelink>, ...]}`); `get_parent_map` and `search_issues` referenced alongside them (acli_graph.py:409 mirror note; search at acli.py:1021+). The real endpoint is `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql` (acli_graph.py:337) — a real Jira Cloud endpoint. Asserting RETURN SHAPE (keys + JSON primitive types) is feasible against these dict/list return types.
- auth/HTTPS preconditions: implicitly satisfied — the live run rides the existing external-marker client builder which constructs `AcliClient(jira_url, user, api_token, jira_project)` from env (test_link_sync_live.py:42-61); HTTPS is the transport of the REST POST.

Finding (one):
- [ ] The "Optional: validate real-client responses against Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI" bullet asserts an external capability whose feasibility is unknown (the plan itself defers spec-completeness to an epic open question). Name this as an explicit SPIKE (investigate-before-commit) rather than an inline Optional bullet, so the core AC1–AC4 harness is not blocked on, or conflated with, an unverified OpenAPI-coverage dependency.
  - criteria: T3
  - location: Plan §What, bullet 4 ("Optional: validate the real client's responses against Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI …")
  - evidence: Plan text "investigate spec completeness first — see epic open question" — an asserted external integration (OpenAPI validation) whose viability is admittedly unknown; routing a capability-gap to a SPIKE is the T3(d) prescription.
  - suggested_fix: Carve the OpenAPI-validation work into a separate SPIKE ticket ("Does Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI cover the comment/issuelinks/parent/search fields well enough to validate response shape?"), and keep this ticket's ACs scoped to the real+fake dual-run harness, which is fully feasible today.
